details: The “Shree Nanaji Deshmukh Awas Yojana” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. In urban areas, where EWS/LIG houses have been allotted by the Local Self-Government Organization/Urban Development Authority and Gujarat Housing Board under the Chief Minister/Prime Minister Awas Yojana, the benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the construction worker registered in the board.
benefits: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹1,60,000/- is provided to registered construction workers.
eligibility: The construction workers who are registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
A house is allotted jointly to a registered construction worker husband or wife and either of the husband or wife is a registered construction worker will be eligible for the benefit of this scheme.
No land/property or building should be in the name of the registered construction worker husband or wife or any of their family members.
The benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the registered construction worker.
The construction worker should be registered in this scheme for at least 2 years.
Application for this scheme has to be made after one year of house allotment.

online: Application Process for ‘E-Nirman Card’ under the GBOCWWB:
Step 01:
The applicant may visit the e-Nirman Portal:
https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/
Step 02:
On the home page, under the tab ‘Login to Portal’, click on ‘
Please Register Here
Step 03:
Enter all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Register’.
Step 04:
After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Step 05:
Now, update your profile by entering all the necessary details as required and click on ‘Update’.
Step 06:
Select the ‘Construction Works Form’ and fill out all the mandatory information in the form.
Step 07:
Upload all the relevant documents, agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the form.
Step 08:
Once the application has been submitted, an application Number is generated which can be kept for future reference.
Process to track Application Status:
On the home page, click on ‘View Citizen Application Status’.
Enter the Application Number and Date of Birth.
Now click on ‘View Status’.
Application Process for Scheme Benefit:
The applicant may visit the Sanman Portal:
https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/
On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on ‘
Enter your Aadhaar Card Number, select user type, and then enter E-Nirman Card Number.
Now, click on ‘Fetch’ & submit the details.
Now, select the scheme and read the instructions carefully for the selected scheme.
Fill out the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Agree with the Rules & Regulations and submit the application form. A confirmation email with the application Number will be sent to the registered email ID.
Post Application Process:
After the application form is submitted by the construction worker, the application will be verified and approved by the District Project Manager.
After that, the application will be verified and approved by the district inspector and sent to the head office.
The State Project Manager of the Head Office will verify the application and send it to the Government Labour Officer (GLO).
After that, the application will be verified and approved by the Government Labour Officer of the Head Office and then by the Member Secretary.
After approval of the application through DD (Demand Draft) assisted by the Accounts Officer. It will be sent to the Authority.

documents_required: Documents required for ‘E-Nirman Card’ under the GBOCWWB:
5. Proof of age (Birth certificate/Driving Licence/PAN Card/Voter ID Card)
5. Letter regarding payment of house instalment
6. Aadhaar Card, Election Card and Residence Proof
7. Copy of rent agreement/rent receipt/letter from landlord
8. Copy of the tax bill of the local municipality

faqs.question: Who is eligible to benefit from this scheme?
faqs.answer: Construction workers who are registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board are eligible to benefit from this scheme.

faqs.question: Can both husband and wife, if registered construction workers, avail the benefit jointly?
faqs.answer: Yes, if a house is allotted jointly to a registered construction worker husband or wife and either of them is a registered construction worker, they are eligible for the scheme's benefits.

faqs.question: Are there any ownership restrictions for eligible applicants?
faqs.answer: Yes, no land/property or building should be in the name of the registered construction worker husband or wife or any of their family members to be eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: How many times can a family avail of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the registered construction worker.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum registration period required to qualify for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the construction worker should be registered in this scheme for at least 2 years to be eligible.

faqs.question: When can an applicant apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Application for this scheme has to be made after one year of house allotment.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹1,60,000/- is provided to registered construction workers.

faqs.question: Can registered construction workers from rural areas avail of this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is specifically for construction workers in urban areas where EWS/LIG houses have been allotted under various housing schemes.

faqs.question: Can applicants apply for this scheme online?
faqs.answer: Yes, the eligible applicant may visit and apply through the Sanman Portal: https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/
